detection.regex: fatal: Authentication failed for '(.+)'|remote: Invalid username or password|could not read Username

solutions.title: Configure GitHub token in CI
solutions.explanation: In CI, GitHub HTTPS authentication requires a token. Use `GITHUB_TOKEN` (automatically provided in GitHub Actions) or a Personal Access Token (PAT) stored as a secret. Add: `git config --global url."https://$TOKEN@github.com/".insteadOf "https://github.com/"`.

solutions.title: Check token permissions
solutions.explanation: The PAT or deploy key may lack required scopes. For GitHub: ensure the token has `repo` (private repos) or `public_repo` (public) scope. Check token expiration.

solutions.title: Switch to SSH authentication
solutions.explanation: Configure an SSH deploy key for the repository (Settings → Deploy keys). Use `git clone git@github.com:org/repo.git` instead of HTTPS. SSH doesn't require token management.

detection.regex: protected branch hook declined|cannot push to protected branch|remote: error: GH006|remote: error: changes must be made through a pull request

solutions.title: Create a pull request instead
solutions.explanation: You attempted to push directly to a protected branch (e.g. `main`, `master`, `production`). The organization requires all changes to go through a Pull Request with code review and CI checks passing.

solutions.title: Check required status checks
solutions.explanation: Even if you create a PR, it may require specific CI checks to pass before merge. Ensure all required status checks in the branch protection rules are satisfied.

detection.regex: CONFLICT \(|Merge conflict in (.+)|Automatic merge failed|fix conflicts and then commit the result

solutions.title: Resolve conflict markers manually
solutions.explanation: Open the conflicted files. Find sections marked with `<<<<<<<`, `=======`, `>>>>>>>` and decide which changes to keep (yours, theirs, or a combination). Then: `git add <file> && git commit`.

solutions.title: Abort merge and try rebase
solutions.command_template: git merge --abort && git pull --rebase origin main
solutions.explanation: Abort the merge with `git merge --abort`, then use `git pull --rebase` instead of merge. Rebase creates a linear history and conflicts are easier to resolve one commit at a time.

solutions.title: Use a visual merge tool
solutions.explanation: Run `git mergetool` to launch a 3-way diff tool (VS Code, vimdiff, meld) to resolve conflicts visually. Configure your preferred tool: `git config --global merge.tool vscode`.

solutions.title: Initialize and update all submodules
solutions.explanation: Submodules are not initialized or are out of date. Run `git submodule update --init --recursive` to initialize and fetch all nested submodules.

solutions.title: Enable submodules in GitHub Actions checkout
solutions.explanation: In GitHub Actions, `actions/checkout` doesn't fetch submodules by default. Add `submodules: recursive` to the checkout step: `uses: actions/checkout@v4 with: submodules: recursive`.

solutions.title: Authenticate for private submodules
solutions.explanation: Private submodule repos require authentication. Configure git credentials before checkout, or use SSH submodule URLs with a deploy key that has access to both the main repo and submodule repos.

detection.regex: batch request failed|LFS object not found|This repository is configured for Git LFS

solutions.title: Install Git LFS
solutions.explanation: Git LFS is not installed or initialized. Install from https://git-lfs.com/ then run `git lfs install` to set up the hooks.

solutions.title: Pull LFS objects explicitly
solutions.explanation: After cloning, LFS files may not be downloaded. Run `git lfs pull` to download all tracked LFS objects. In CI, use `GIT_LFS_SKIP_SMUDGE=0` to ensure LFS files are downloaded.

solutions.title: Enable LFS in GitHub Actions
solutions.explanation: Add `lfs: true` to the checkout step: `uses: actions/checkout@v4 with: lfs: true`. This automatically runs `git lfs pull` after checkout.

patterns.name: Git Shallow Clone Update Not Allowed

detection.regex: shallow update not allowed|fatal: shallow file has changed since we read it

solutions.title: Convert to full clone
solutions.explanation: The repository was cloned with `--depth=1` (shallow), which prevents rebase and some merge operations. Convert to a full clone: `git fetch --unshallow`.

solutions.title: Increase fetch depth in CI
solutions.explanation: In GitHub Actions, change `fetch-depth: 1` to `fetch-depth: 0` for full history. If you need history for versioning (git-describe, release notes), always use `fetch-depth: 0`.

solutions.title: Fetch missing tags
solutions.explanation: Shallow clones also miss tags. If your build process needs git tags (e.g. for version numbering), use `git fetch --tags --unshallow`.

detection.regex: Unable to create '.*/refs/heads/.*\.lock'|Could not create directory.*\.git

solutions.title: Remove stale lock files
solutions.explanation: A stale `.lock` file was left by a crashed or killed git process. Remove it: `find .git -name '*.lock' -delete`. This is safe if no other git process is currently running.

solutions.title: Kill other concurrent git processes
solutions.explanation: If another git process is running (CI parallel jobs, IDEs), wait for it to finish. Running two git write operations concurrently in the same repo causes lock conflicts.

solutions.title: Fix .git directory permissions
solutions.explanation: If the `.git` directory is not writable, git cannot create lock files (and cannot write at all). Fix with `chmod -R 755 .git`.

solutions.title: Pull and rebase before pushing
solutions.command_template: git pull --rebase origin main && git push
solutions.explanation: The remote has new commits that aren't in your local branch (non-fast-forward). Pull and rebase your changes on top: `git pull --rebase origin main`, resolve any conflicts, then push.

solutions.title: Force push with safety check
solutions.explanation: If you intentionally rewrote history (rebase, amend), use `--force-with-lease` instead of `--force`. It fails if someone else pushed in the meantime, preventing accidental overwrites.

solutions.title: Check branch protection rules
solutions.explanation: The rejection may be due to branch protection (required reviews, status checks). See `git_branch_protection` pattern. Create a PR instead of pushing directly.
